old_claim.note: Scientific Method provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No scientific-method dependency remains because Crown invention privileges were legal grants already documented in 1449, before the repository's 1500 scientific-method node.

source_shape.source_locator: Sections 1-3 date the English and Welsh patent records from 1449 and describe a petition, warrant, bill, and sealing process rather than experimental method.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The early patent mechanism was an administrative and legal grant process documented in fifteenth-century records.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A broad history of the later Scientific Revolution neither supports the grant date nor establishes experiment as a condition of the privilege.

ontology_before: A fifteenth-century legal grant depended on a later generalized scientific-method node through an unrelated source.
ontology_after: English invention privileges remain a legal-institutional root; scientific practice is not made a grant prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: early_patent_systems no longer directly depends on scientific_method.
would_reject_if: A primary grant rule required experimental scientific method for the scoped 1449 privilege.
short_reason: The legal grants predate and do not require scientific method.
